Haiden | Location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Muroran City|ja|室蘭市}}, Miyano Mori-chō 1-chōme 1-ban 64 | Position = {{coord|42|21|18|N|141|1|18|E|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | Founded = {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year }} (Meiji 23) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|August 5|ja|8月5日}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=21|lat_sec=18|lon_deg=141|lon_min=1|lon_sec=18|label=中嶋神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Nakajima Jinja''' (中嶋神社【なかじまじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Muroran City, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former shrine rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In {{ill|1887|ja|1887th year }} (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In {{ill|1889|ja|1889th year }} (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year }} (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In {{ill|1895|ja|1895th year }} (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|ja|黒田清隆}}, was offered. * In {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year }} (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the {{ill|7th Division|ja|第7師団}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year }} (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year }} (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In {{ill|1939|ja|1939th year }} (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In {{ill|1977|ja|1977th year }} (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year }} (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990th year }} (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishimahime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden | Location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|12=draft}} {{ill|Muroran City|ja|室蘭市|12=draft}}, Miyano Mori-chō 1-chōme 1-ban 64 | Position = {{coord|42|21|18|N|141|1|18|E|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=draft}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|12=draft}} | Founded = {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 23) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|August 5|ja|8月5日|12=draft}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=21|lat_sec=18|lon_deg=141|lon_min=1|lon_sec=18|label=中嶋神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Nakajima Jinja''' (中嶋神社【なかじまじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Muroran City, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|12=draft}}. Its former shrine rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=draft}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=draft}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In {{ill|1887|ja|1887th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In {{ill|1889|ja|1889th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In {{ill|1895|ja|1895th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|ja|黒田清隆|12=draft}}, was offered. * In {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the {{ill|7th Division|ja|第7師団|12=draft}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft}} (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In {{ill|1939|ja|1939th year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In {{ill|1977|ja|1977th year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990th year|12=draft}} (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishimahime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)}}

Its former shrine rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=draft}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=draft}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In {{ill|1887|ja|1887th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In {{ill|1889|ja|1889th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In {{ill|1895|ja|1895th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|ja|黒田清隆|12=draft}}, was offered. * In {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the {{ill|7th Division|ja|第7師団|12=draft}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft}} (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In {{ill|1939|ja|1939th year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In {{ill|1977|ja|1977th year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990th year|12=draft}} (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishimahime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)}}

{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | Founded = {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year}} (Meiji 23) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|August 5|ja|8月5日}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=21|lat_sec=18|lon_deg=141|lon_min=1|lon_sec=18|label=中嶋神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Nakajima Jinja''' (中嶋神社【なかじまじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Muroran City, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In {{ill|1887|ja|1887th year}} (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In {{ill|1889|ja|1889th year}} (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year}} (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In {{ill|1895|ja|1895th year}} (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|ja|黒田清隆}}, was offered. * In {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year}} (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the {{ill|7th Division|ja|第7師団}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year}} (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year}} (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In {{ill|1939|ja|1939th year}} (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In {{ill|1977|ja|1977th year}} (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year}} (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990th year}} (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishimahime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)}}

At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In {{ill|1895|ja|1895th year}} (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|ja|黒田清隆}}, was offered. * In {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year}} (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the {{ill|7th Division|ja|第7師団}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year}} (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year}} (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In {{ill|1939|ja|1939th year}} (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In {{ill|1977|ja|1977th year}} (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year}} (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990th year}} (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishimahime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. revision.comment: 7 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市) up to rev 97857357
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 中嶋神社 |画像 = [[File:中嶋神社 室蘭.jpg|270px|室蘭市の中島神社]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|21|18|N|141|1|18|E}}}} |所在地 = [[北海道]][[室蘭市]]宮の森町1丁目1番64号 |位置= {{coord|42|21|18|N|141|1|18|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[天照皇大神]]
[[大国主神]]
[[事代主神]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[1890年]]（[[明治]]23年） |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[8月5日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=21|lat_sec=18|lon_deg=141|lon_min=1|lon_sec=18|label=中嶋神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''中嶋神社'''（なかじまじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[室蘭市]]にある[[神社]]。旧社格は[[村社]]。 神社に隣接して各種催しや講演会、宴会や結婚式場に利用されている蓬崍殿（ほうらいでん）がある。 == 祭神 == * [[天照皇大神]]（あまてらすすめおおかみ） * [[大国主神]]（おおくにぬしのかみ） * [[事代主神]]（ことしろぬしのかみ） == 歴史 == * [[1887年]]（[[明治]]20年）、輪西に[[屯田兵]]第1次入植（110戸）。 * [[1889年]]（明治22年）、屯田兵第2次入植（110戸）。 * [[1890年]]（明治23年）、圓山（まるやま。現在の輪西町と東町の間で新日鉄住金球場の裏山{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=室蘭市 |accessdate=2015-08-07}}）に社殿を建立（「'''兵村社又圓山神社'''」）。 * [[1895年]]（明治28年）、元開拓長官[[黒田清隆]]揮毫の[[神 (神道)|神号]]奉納。 * [[1897年]]（明治30年）、屯田兵の中隊本部が[[第7師団 (日本軍)|第7師団]]司令部に吸収移管。跡地となる現在地（旧中島台）に遷座し「'''中嶋神社'''」と奉称。 * [[1909年]]（明治42年）、[[北海道炭礦汽船]]輪西製鐵場（現在の[[新日鐵住金室蘭製鐵所]]）創業記念大国主神、事代主神鋳造像奉納。配神として奉斎。 * [[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年）、供進指定村社に列格。 * [[1939年]]（[[昭和]]14年）、社殿造営。 * [[1977年]]（昭和52年）、神明造内宮型に造営。 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）、輪西屯田入植百周年記念慰霊祭執行。屯田記念誌発行。 * [[1990年]]（[[平成]]2年）、境内整備と神社史を発行。 == 摂末社 == : 輪西稲荷神社（わにしいなりじんじゃ） : 宇迦之御魂神（うかのみたまのかみ） : 佐田彦神（さたひこのかみ） : 大宮能売神（おおみやのめのかみ） : 厳島神社（いつくしまじんじゃ） : 市杵島姫神（いちきしまひめのかみ） : 田心姫神（たごりひめのかみ） : 湍津姫神（たぎつひめのかみ） : 相馬妙見神社（そうまみょうけんじんじゃ） : 天御中主神稲荷神社（あめのみなかぬしのかみいなりじんじゃ） : 宇迦之御魂神（うかのみたまのかみ） == 祭事 == : 例大祭 :: 毎年8月4日・5日に開かれ、宵宮祭（4日）、本祭（5日）が行われて露店が軒を連ねる。また、境内にある室蘭市弓道場では「奉納弓道大会」（5日）なども行われる。 == 文化財 == : 輪西屯田兵旧火薬庫（室蘭市指定有形文化財）{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=室蘭市 |accessdate=2015-08-07}} : 輪西屯田兵記念碑（室蘭市指定有形文化財） : 輪西屯田兵関係資料（室蘭市指定民俗文化財） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[北海道の神社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/12/12003.html 中嶋神社（北海道神社庁）] * [http://houraiden.or.jp/ 蓬崍殿] ** {{Facebook|houraiden.main|蓬崍殿・ハートフルパーク}} {{デフォルトソート:なかしましんしやむろらんし}} [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧村社]] [[Category:室蘭市の建築物]] [[Category:室蘭市の歴史]]

|accessdate=2015-08-07}}）に社殿を建立（「'''兵村社又圓山神社'''」）。 * [[1895年]]（明治28年）、元開拓長官[[黒田清隆]]揮毫の[[神 (神道)|神号]]奉納。 * [[1897年]]（明治30年）、屯田兵の中隊本部が[[第7師団 (日本軍)|第7師団]]司令部に吸収移管。跡地となる現在地（旧中島台）に遷座し「'''中嶋神社'''」と奉称。 * [[1909年]]（明治42年）、[[北海道炭礦汽船]]輪西製鐵場（現在の[[新日鐵住金室蘭製鐵所]]）創業記念大国主神、事代主神鋳造像奉納。配神として奉斎。 * [[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年）、供進指定村社に列格。 * [[1939年]]（[[昭和]]14年）、社殿造営。 * [[1977年]]（昭和52年）、神明造内宮型に造営。 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）、輪西屯田入植百周年記念慰霊祭執行。屯田記念誌発行。 * [[1990年]]（[[平成]]2年）、境内整備と神社史を発行。 == 摂末社 == : 輪西稲荷神社（わにしいなりじんじゃ） : 宇迦之御魂神（うかのみたまのかみ） : 佐田彦神（さたひこのかみ） : 大宮能売神（おおみやのめのかみ） : 厳島神社（いつくしまじんじゃ） : 市杵島姫神（いちきしまひめのかみ） : 田心姫神（たごりひめのかみ） : 湍津姫神（たぎつひめのかみ） : 相馬妙見神社（そうまみょうけんじんじゃ） : 天御中主神稲荷神社（あめのみなかぬしのかみいなりじんじゃ） : 宇迦之御魂神（うかのみたまのかみ） == 祭事 == : 例大祭 :: 毎年8月4日・5日に開かれ、宵宮祭（4日）、本祭（5日）が行われて露店が軒を連ねる。また、境内にある室蘭市弓道場では「奉納弓道大会」（5日）なども行われる。 == 文化財 == : 輪西屯田兵旧火薬庫（室蘭市指定有形文化財）{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=室蘭市 |accessdate=2015-08-07}} : 輪西屯田兵記念碑（室蘭市指定有形文化財） : 輪西屯田兵関係資料（室蘭市指定民俗文化財） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[北海道の神社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/12/12003.html 中嶋神社（北海道神社庁）] * [http://houraiden.or.jp/ 蓬崍殿] ** {{Facebook|houraiden.main|蓬崍殿・ハートフルパーク}} {{デフォルトソート:なかしましんしやむろらんし}} [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:北海道の旧村社]] [[Category:室蘭市の建築物]] [[Category:室蘭市の歴史]]

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Nakajima Jinja (中嶋神社) | image = [[File:中嶋神社 室蘭.jpg|270px|室蘭市の中島神社]]
Haiden | Location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}} {{ill|Muroran City|ja|室蘭市}}, Miyano Mori-chō 1-chōme 1-ban 64 | Position = {{coord|42|21|18|N|141|1|18|E|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} | Founded = {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year}} (Meiji 23) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|August 5|ja|8月5日}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=21|lat_sec=18|lon_deg=141|lon_min=1|lon_sec=18|label=中嶋神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Nakajima Jinja''' (中嶋神社【なかじまじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Muroran City, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In {{ill|1887|ja|1887th year}} (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In {{ill|1889|ja|1889th year}} (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year}} (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In {{ill|1895|ja|1895th year}} (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|ja|黒田清隆}}, was offered. * In {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year}} (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the {{ill|7th Division|ja|第7師団}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year}} (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year}} (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In {{ill|1939|ja|1939th year}} (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In {{ill|1977|ja|1977th year}} (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year}} (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990th year}} (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishimahime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)}} {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}}

In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Nakajima Jinja (中嶋神社) | image = [[File:中嶋神社 室蘭.jpg|270px|室蘭市の中島神社]]
Haiden | Location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|12=draft}} {{ill|Muroran City|ja|室蘭市|12=draft}}, Miyano Mori-chō 1-chōme 1-ban 64 | Position = {{coord|42|21|18|N|141|1|18|E|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=draft}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|12=draft}} | Founded = {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 23) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|August 5|ja|8月5日|12=draft}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=21|lat_sec=18|lon_deg=141|lon_min=1|lon_sec=18|label=中嶋神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Nakajima Jinja''' (中嶋神社【なかじまじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Muroran City, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|12=draft}}. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=draft}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=draft}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In {{ill|1887|ja|1887th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In {{ill|1889|ja|1889th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In {{ill|1895|ja|1895th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|ja|黒田清隆|12=draft}}, was offered. * In {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the {{ill|7th Division|ja|第7師団|12=draft}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft}} (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In {{ill|1939|ja|1939th year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In {{ill|1977|ja|1977th year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990th year|12=draft}} (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Nakajima Jinja (中嶋神社) | image = [[File:中嶋神社 室蘭.jpg|270px|室蘭市の中島神社]]
Haiden | Location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Muroran City|ja|室蘭市|qq=draft}}, Miyano Mori-chō 1-chōme 1-ban 64 | Position = {{coord|42|21|18|N|141|1|18|E|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft}} | Founded = {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year|qq=draft}} (Meiji 23) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|August 5|ja|8月5日|qq=draft}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=21|lat_sec=18|lon_deg=141|lon_min=1|lon_sec=18|label=中嶋神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Nakajima Jinja''' (中嶋神社【なかじまじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Muroran City, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft}}. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In {{ill|1887|ja|1887th year|qq=draft}} (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In {{ill|1889|ja|1889th year|qq=draft}} (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year|qq=draft}} (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In {{ill|1895|ja|1895th year|qq=draft}} (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|ja|黒田清隆|qq=draft}}, was offered. * In {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=draft}} (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the {{ill|7th Division|ja|第7師団|qq=draft}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year|qq=draft}} (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|qq=draft}} (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In {{ill|1939|ja|1939th year|qq=draft}} (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In {{ill|1977|ja|1977th year|qq=draft}} (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year|qq=draft}} (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990th year|qq=draft}} (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Nakajima Jinja (中嶋神社) | image = [[File:中嶋神社 室蘭.jpg|270px|室蘭市の中島神社]]
Haiden | Location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Muroran City|ja|室蘭市|qq=draft}}, Miyano Mori-chō 1-chōme 1-ban 64 | Position = {{coord|42|21|18|N|141|1|18|E|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft}} | Founded = {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year|qq=draft}} (Meiji 23) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|August 5|ja|8月5日|qq=draft}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=21|lat_sec=18|lon_deg=141|lon_min=1|lon_sec=18|label=中嶋神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Nakajima Jinja''' (中嶋神社【なかじまじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Muroran City, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft}}. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In {{ill|1887|ja|1887th year|qq=draft}} (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In {{ill|1889|ja|1889th year|qq=draft}} (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year|qq=draft}} (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In {{ill|1895|ja|1895th year|qq=draft}} (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|ja|黒田清隆|qq=draft}}, was offered. * In {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=draft}} (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the {{ill|7th Division|ja|第7師団|qq=draft}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year|qq=draft}} (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|qq=draft}} (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In {{ill|1939|ja|1939th year|qq=draft}} (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In {{ill|1977|ja|1977th year|qq=draft}} (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year|qq=draft}} (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990th year|qq=draft}} (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

former Director of the Development Administration, {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|ja|黒田清隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kiyotaka|qq=}}, was offered. * In {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1897|qq=}} (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the {{ill|7th Division|ja|第7師団|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/7th Division|qq=}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1917|qq=}} (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In {{ill|1939|ja|1939th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1939|qq=}} (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In {{ill|1977|ja|1977th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1977|qq=}} (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1987|qq=}} (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1990|qq=}} (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Nakajima Jinja (中嶋神社) | image = [[File:中嶋神社 室蘭.jpg|270px|室蘭市の中島神社]]
Haiden | Location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}} {{ill|Muroran City|ja|室蘭市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroran City|qq=}}, Miyano Mori-chō 1-chōme 1-ban 64 | Position = {{coord|42|21|18|N|141|1|18|E|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=}} | Founded = {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1890|qq=}} (Meiji 23) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|August 5|ja|8月5日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 5|qq=}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=21|lat_sec=18|lon_deg=141|lon_min=1|lon_sec=18|label=中嶋神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Nakajima Jinja''' (中嶋神社【なかじまじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Muroran City, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}}. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In {{ill|1887|ja|1887th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1887|qq=}} (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In {{ill|1889|ja|1889th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1889|qq=}} (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1890|qq=}} (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In {{ill|1895|ja|1895th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1895|qq=}} (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|ja|黒田清隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kiyotaka|qq=}}, was offered. * In {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1897|qq=}} (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the {{ill|7th Division|ja|第7師団|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/7th Division|qq=}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1917|qq=}} (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In {{ill|1939|ja|1939th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1939|qq=}} (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In {{ill|1977|ja|1977th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1977|qq=}} (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1987|qq=}} (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1990|qq=}} (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]]

Haiden | Location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}} {{ill|Muroran City|ja|室蘭市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroran City|qq=}}, Miyano Mori-chō 1-chōme 1-ban 64 | Position = {{coord|42|21|18|N|141|1|18|E|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=}} | Founded = {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1890|qq=}} (Meiji 23) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = {{ill|August 5|ja|8月5日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 5|qq=}} | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=21|lat_sec=18|lon_deg=141|lon_min=1|lon_sec=18|label=中嶋神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Nakajima Jinja''' (中嶋神社【なかじまじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Muroran City, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}}. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In {{ill|1887|ja|1887th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1887|qq=}} (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In {{ill|1889|ja|1889th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1889|qq=}} (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In {{ill|1890|ja|1890th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1890|qq=}} (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In {{ill|1895|ja|1895th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1895|qq=}} (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|ja|黒田清隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kiyotaka|qq=}}, was offered. * In {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1897|qq=}} (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the {{ill|7th Division|ja|第7師団|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/7th Division|qq=}}, the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1917|qq=}} (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In {{ill|1939|ja|1939th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1939|qq=}} (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In {{ill|1977|ja|1977th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1977|qq=}} (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1987|qq=}} (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1990|qq=}} (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]]
