Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In [[:en:1887|1887]] (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In [[:en:1895|1895]] (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]], was offered. * In [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the [[:en:7th Division|7th Division]], the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In [[:en:1977|1977]] (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]]

Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In [[:en:1887|1887]] (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=}} | Founded = [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23) | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:August 5|August 5]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=21|lat_sec=18|lon_deg=141|lon_min=1|lon_sec=18|label=中嶋神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Nakajima Jinja''' (中嶋神社【なかじまじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Muroran City, [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In [[:en:1887|1887]] (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In [[:en:1895|1895]] (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]], was offered. * In [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the [[:en:7th Division|7th Division]], the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In [[:en:1977|1977]] (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]]

Haiden | Location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Muroran|Muroran City]], Miyano Mori-chō 1-chōme 1-ban 64 | Position = {{coord|42|21|18|N|141|1|18|E|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=}} | Founded = [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23) | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:August 5|August 5]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=21|lat_sec=18|lon_deg=141|lon_min=1|lon_sec=18|label=中嶋神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Nakajima Jinja''' (中嶋神社【なかじまじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Muroran City, [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In [[:en:1887|1887]] (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In [[:en:1895|1895]] (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]], was offered. * In [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the [[:en:7th Division|7th Division]], the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In [[:en:1977|1977]] (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:室蘭市の建築物]] [[Category:室蘭市の歴史]]

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Nakajima Jinja (中嶋神社) | image = [[File:中嶋神社 室蘭.jpg|270px|室蘭市の中島神社]]
Haiden | Location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Muroran|Muroran City]], Miyano Mori-chō 1-chōme 1-ban 64 | Position = {{coord|42|21|18|N|141|1|18|E|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Founded = [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23) | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:August 5|August 5]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=21|lat_sec=18|lon_deg=141|lon_min=1|lon_sec=18|label=中嶋神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Nakajima Jinja''' (中嶋神社【なかじまじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Muroran City, [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In [[:en:1887|1887]] (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In [[:en:1895|1895]] (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]], was offered. * In [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the [[:en:7th Division|7th Division]], the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In [[:en:1977|1977]] (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Mibu Town]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Muroran]] [[Category:室蘭市の歴史]]

Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mibu Town]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Muroran]] [[Category:室蘭市の歴史]]

Haiden | Location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Muroran|Muroran City]], Miyano Mori-chō 1-chōme 1-ban 64 | Position = {{coord|42|21|18|N|141|1|18|E|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Founded = [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23) | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:August 5|August 5]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=21|lat_sec=18|lon_deg=141|lon_min=1|lon_sec=18|label=中嶋神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Nakajima Jinja''' (中嶋神社【なかじまじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Muroran City, [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In [[:en:1887|1887]] (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In [[:en:1895|1895]] (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]], was offered. * In [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the [[:en:7th Division|7th Division]], the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In [[:en:1977|1977]] (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Designated Tangible Cultural Properties in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotsuki Clan]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Muroran]] [[Category:室蘭市の歴史]]

{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Founded = [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23) | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:August 5|August 5]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=21|lat_sec=18|lon_deg=141|lon_min=1|lon_sec=18|label=中嶋神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Nakajima Jinja''' (中嶋神社【なかじまじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Muroran City, [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In [[:en:1887|1887]] (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In [[:en:1895|1895]] (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]], was offered. * In [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the [[:en:7th Division|7th Division]], the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In [[:en:1977|1977]] (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Muroran]] [[Category:室蘭市の歴史]]

Haiden | Location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Muroran|Muroran City]], Miyano Mori-chō 1-chōme 1-ban 64 | Position = {{coord|42|21|18|N|141|1|18|E|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}

{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Founded = [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23) | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:August 5|August 5]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=21|lat_sec=18|lon_deg=141|lon_min=1|lon_sec=18|label=中嶋神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Nakajima Jinja''' (中嶋神社【なかじまじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Muroran City, [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In [[:en:1887|1887]] (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In [[:en:1895|1895]] (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]], was offered. * In [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the [[:en:7th Division|7th Division]], the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In [[:en:1977|1977]] (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Muroran]] [[Category:室蘭市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:室蘭の Municipal History]] into [[Category:Municipal History of Muroran]] via bot
revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Nakajima Jinja (中嶋神社) | image = [[File:中嶋神社 室蘭.jpg|270px|室蘭市の中島神社]]
Haiden | Location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Muroran|Muroran City]], Miyano Mori-chō 1-chōme 1-ban 64 | Position = {{coord|42|21|18|N|141|1|18|E|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Founded = [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23) | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:August 5|August 5]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=21|lat_sec=18|lon_deg=141|lon_min=1|lon_sec=18|label=中嶋神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Nakajima Jinja''' (中嶋神社【なかじまじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Muroran City, [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In [[:en:1887|1887]] (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In [[:en:1895|1895]] (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]], was offered. * In [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the [[:en:7th Division|7th Division]], the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In [[:en:1977|1977]] (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Muroran]] [[Category:Municipal History of Muroran]]

Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In [[:en:1887|1887]] (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In [[:en:1895|1895]] (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]], was offered. * In [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the [[:en:7th Division|7th Division]], the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In [[:en:1977|1977]] (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Muroran]] [[Category:Municipal History of Muroran]]

Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In [[:en:1887|1887]] (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In [[:en:1895|1895]] (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]], was offered. * In [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the [[:en:7th Division|7th Division]], the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In [[:en:1977|1977]] (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Muroran]] [[Category:Municipal History of Muroran]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q21866371]] Wikidata name: Nakajima-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Nakajima Jinja (中嶋神社) | image = File:中嶋神社 室蘭.jpg | Location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Muroran|Muroran City]], Miyano Mori-chō 1-chōme 1-ban 64 | Position = {{coord|42|21|18|N|141|1|18|E|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}

{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} | Divine Object = | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Founded = [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23) | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = | Place Marker = | Annual Festival = [[:en:August 5|August 5]] | Shinto Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=21|lat_sec=18|lon_deg=141|lon_min=1|lon_sec=18|label=中嶋神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location = {{ill|Muroran|qid=Q496667}} | coordinates = {{Coord|42.355|N|141.02166666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Nakajima Jinja''' (中嶋神社【なかじまじんじゃ】) is a Shinto shrine located in Muroran City, [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In [[:en:1887|1887]] (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In [[:en:1895|1895]] (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]], was offered. * In [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the [[:en:7th Division|7th Division]], the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In [[:en:1977|1977]] (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Muroran]] [[Category:Municipal History of Muroran]]

Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In [[:en:1887|1887]] (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In [[:en:1895|1895]] (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]], was offered. * In [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the [[:en:7th Division|7th Division]], the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In [[:en:1977|1977]] (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]]}} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Muroran]] [[Category:Municipal History of Muroran]]

Its former shrine Rank is that of a village shrine. Adjacent to the shrine is the Hōraiden (蓬崍殿), which is used for various events such as lectures, banquets, and wedding ceremonies. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōkuninushi) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Kotoshironushi) == History == * In [[:en:1887|1887]] (Meiji 20), the First Tondenhei (屯田兵) settlement was established in Wansei. * In [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22), the Second Tondenhei settlement was established (110 households). * In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a shrine building was erected on Maruyama (currently located between Wansei-chō and Higashi-chō behind the Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Stadium, as referenced in the "Furusato Muroran Guidebook" {{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org1400/documents/zenpe.pdf |title=ふるさと室蘭ガイドブック |page=56 |format=PDF |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}}). At that time, it was known as “Hyōsonsha Mata Maruyama Jinja” (兵村社又圓山神社). * In [[:en:1895|1895]] (Meiji 28), a kami inscription calligraphy by former Director of the Development Administration, [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]], was offered. * In [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), after the Tondenhei company headquarters was absorbed into the command of the [[:en:7th Division|7th Division]], the shrine was relocated to its present site (the former Nakajimadai) and was thereafter named “Nakajima Jinja.” * In [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42), a cast bronze statue of Ōkuninushi and Kotoshironushi was donated to commemorate the founding of the Hokkaido Coal and Steamship Wansei Steel Works (currently Nippon Steel & Sumitomo Metal Muroran Steel Works) and enshrined as subsidiary deities. * In [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6), it was classified as a designated village shrine. * In [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14), a new shrine building was constructed. * In [[:en:1977|1977]] (Shōwa 52), the shrine was rebuilt in the Shinmei-zukuri style. * In [[:en:1987|1987]] (Shōwa 62), a memorial service was held to mark the 100th anniversary of the Wansei Tondenhei settlement, accompanied by the publication of a Tonden Memorial Record. * In [[:en:1990|1990]] (Heisei 2), the shrine grounds were renovated and a shrine history was published. == Sessoku-sha (Subordinate Shrines) == : Wansei Inari Jinja (輪西稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) : Satahiko no Kami (佐田彦神) : Ōmiya Nome no Kami (大宮能売神) : Itsukushima Jinja (厳島神社) : Ichikishima-hime no Kami (市杵島姫神) : Tagokori-hime no Kami (田心姫神) : Tagitsu-hime no Kami (湍津姫神) : Sōma Myōken Jinja (相馬妙見神社) : Ame-no-Minakanushi Inari Jinja (天御中主神稲荷神社) : Ukano Mitama no Kami (宇迦之御魂神) == Festivals == : Grand Festival (例大祭) – Held annually on August 4 and 5, featuring an evening festival (宵宮祭) on the 4th and the main festival (本祭) on the 5th, with numerous food stalls lining the shrine grounds. In addition, the Muroran City Kyūdōjō (archery range) on the shrine premises hosts events such as the “Hōnō Kyūdō Tournament” on the 5th. == Cultural Properties == : Wansei Tondenhei Former Ammunition Warehouse (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City){{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.city.muroran.lg.jp/main/org9420/muroran_bunkazai.html |title=室蘭市の文化財 |publisher=Muroran City|accessdate=2015-08-07}} : Wansei Tondenhei Memorial Monument (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Muroran City) : Materials Related to the Wansei Tondenhei (Designated as Folk Cultural Properties by Muroran City) == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Shrine Historical Documents * Shrine Grounds Explanation Board * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編纂|year=1912|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/651}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), pages 651–652. * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|year=1988|chapter=Ogoto Jinja|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9: Tochigi Prefecture Place Names|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|雄琴神社（平凡社）|1988th year }}}} == Related Articles == * [[Ogoto Jinja (Ōtsu City)|Ogoto Jinja]] (Ogoto Jinja in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture) – the source shrine for the enshrinement. == External Links == * [http://kir579053.kir.jp/jinjyacyo/?p=759 Ogoto Jinja] - Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Office {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|中嶋神社 (室蘭市)|version=97857357|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]]}} [[sn:Nakajima Shrine (Muroran)]] [[ja:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[sn:中嶋神社 (室蘭市)]] [[ja:中嶋神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Muroran]] [[Category:Municipal History of Muroran]]
